title: Moody’s Lifts Argentina Debt Rating in Fresh Boost to Milei
summary: Moody’s Ratings upgraded Argentina’s credit score, giving the nation its third sovereign upgrade in less than three months and further validating President Javier Milei’s economic overhaul plan.

title: Banks Wrap Up Loan Backing SoftBank’s $5.4 Billion Robotics Bet
summary: A group of banks wrapped up a loan offering backing SoftBank Group Corp.’s $5.4 billion robotics bet after increasing the size of the US dollar tranche.

title: This ETF Tax Loophole Is All the Rage Among Rich Americans
summary: A bespoke fund is becoming a must-have tool for wealthy investors looking to defer — and sometimes even avoid — capital gains tax.

title: Bitcoin Rallies After Bessent Says Clarity Act at ‘1-Yard Line’
summary: Crypto markets are stirring after months of listless trading on signs that Washington is moving toward a clearer rulebook for digital assets.

title: Alaska Airlines Narrowly Beats Estimates as Fuel Costs Stay High
summary: Alaska Air Group Inc. earnings outlook slightly beat analyst predictions as the airline grapples with rising fuel prices from the war in the Middle East.

title: High Yielders Pace Emerging-Market Currency Gains, Stocks Jump
summary: Emerging-market currencies were mixed on Tuesday, with high-yielding names outperforming as investors looked past the latest developments in the Middle East and focused on carry trades instead.

title: Stocks Climb as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, boosting stocks amid a rally in chipmakers on bets the artificial-intelligence trade that’s powered the bull market has more room to run.

title: Nasdaq 100 Notches Best Day in Weeks as Chipmakers Rebound
summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index posted its best day in three weeks on Tuesday as a rebound in chipmakers at the center of the artificial-intelligence trade overshadowed the re-escalation in the US-Iran war.

title: Capital One Profit Beats Estimates as Loan-Loss Provisions Drop
summary: Capital One Financial Corp., the biggest US credit-card lender, swung to a profit in the second quarter as it set aside less money to cover bad loans than Wall Street had anticipated.

title: Canadian Stocks Climb as Gold Surge Offsets Trump Tariff Threat
summary: Canadian stocks climbed Tuesday despite US President Donald Trump threatening 50% tariffs on the country’s exports, thanks to surging gold prices and a rally in tech hardware stocks.

title: US Yields Hit Two-Month High as Oil Sparks Inflation Risk
summary: US Treasuries fell, pushing 10- and 30-year yields to the highest in about two months, as a surge in oil prices stoked concern that inflationary pressures will prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
summary: Brent oil advanced to the highest since early June as traders weighed threats to maritime traffic that span from the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea to a key Kazakh export terminal on Russia’s coast.

title: Gold and Silver Rise on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Fed Strategy
summary: Gold and silver advanced on dip-buying as traders monitored Middle East tensions for signs that higher energy costs could stoke inflation, putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten policy.

title: Treasury Flags Concern Over ‘Potentially Abusive’ Tax Trades
summary: The US Treasury Department has expressed concern over a number of high-profile tax strategies touted by Wall Street that it says may be “too good to be true.”

title: BlackRock vs BlackRock: Aston Martin Debt Revamp Risks In-House Clash
summary: A potential debt revamp at Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc is setting the stage for an unusual battle between different divisions of asset management giant BlackRock Inc.

title: Subprime Auto Dealer Goes From Covid-Era Rockstar to Near Demise
summary: America’s Car-Mart Inc. thrived during a ’90s slump, the 2008 financial crisis and the upheaval of Covid-19 by doing one thing well: selling and financing older, high-mileage used cars for cash-strapped, deeply-subprime buyers.

title: Iberdrola Agrees to Buy 80% Stake in Caruna for €2 Billion
summary: Iberdrola SA agreed to buy an 80% stake in Finland’s main electricity distribution network Caruna Group for €2 billion ($2.3 billion).

title: Colombia, Peru Leaders Draw Investors With Policy Signals
summary: Investors are finding fresh reasons to buy Colombian and Peruvian assets as the countries’ newly elected presidents deliver early signals that their market-friendly agendas will translate into policy overhaul.

title: Liverpool FC Owners Consider Selling Stake to Bhatia-Led Group
summary: The owners of Liverpool FC are considering selling a stake in the English Premier League football club to a consortium of investors led by Amit Bhatia.

title: Oaktree’s Poli Sees a $200 Billion ‘Distressed Moment’ Coming as Rates Stay High
summary: Companies with $200 billion in debt face a reckoning as funding costs stay high and debt comes due over the next few years, according to distressed specialist Oaktree Capital Management.
